rules.name: discarded-error
rules.message: Explicitly discarded error return value — may hide failures.
rules.fix_hint: Handle the error: check with if err != nil or wrap with fmt.Errorf.

rules.name: fmt-print-in-non-test
rules.message: fmt.Print in non-test code — use a structured logger instead.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with log/slog or a structured logging library (e.g. zap, zerolog).

rules.name: hardcoded-credentials
rules.fix_hint: Move secrets to environment variables or a vault; load via os.Getenv or a config library.

rules.name: panic-usage
rules.message: panic() usage — crashes the entire program; prefer returning errors.
rules.fix_hint: Return an error instead of panicking; reserve panic for truly unrecoverable situations in init.

rules.name: time-sleep
rules.message: time.Sleep call — acceptable in Go but verify it is intentional.
rules.fix_hint: Consider using a ticker, timer, or context-based cancellation for more responsive shutdown.

rules.name: deep-nesting
rules.message: Deep nesting detected (4+ indent levels) — reduces readability.
rules.fix_hint: Extract inner logic into named functions or use early returns / guard clauses.

rules.name: magic-numbers
rules.message: Magic number in comparison — extract to a named constant for clarity.
rules.fix_hint: Define a package-level const with a descriptive name.

file_size._note: Lines of code. Go files tend to be compact; test files get more room.

function_size._note: Go functions can be longer due to explicit error handling.
